title: Conflict at work
paperName: Paper 3 - Specialist Options: Approaches, Issues and Debates
summary: 9990 Organisational — sources of workplace conflict and resolution strategies.
learningObjectives: Identify sources: role ambiguity, scarce resources, personality.

simpleExplanation.title: When colleagues clash
simpleExplanation.summary: Workplace conflict arises from competing goals and unclear roles — managed well it sparks innovation; managed badly it destroys morale.

flashcards.back: Functional (constructive) stimulates ideas and clarifies roles; dysfunctional destroys morale, cohesion, and performance.
High assertiveness, low cooperativeness — win-lose, useful in emergencies but damages relationships if overused.
High assertiveness and cooperativeness — integrative problem-solving, best when both sides' interests matter.
Moderate both — split the difference; quick but may leave both partially dissatisfied.
Role ambiguity/overlap, scarce resources, personality clashes, incompatible goals, unfair treatment.
Mediator facilitates agreement (parties keep control); arbitrator imposes a binding decision.

sections.content: **Conflict at work** (4.3.3) completes 4.3. Apply **Thomas-Kilmann modes** to scenarios — name the mode and justify when it fits.

sections.question: Marketing and production clash over a product launch date. Marketing wants early release for a trade fair; production says quality testing needs six more weeks. Tension is rising and emails have become personal. Analyse sources of conflict and recommend a resolution approach.
sections.solution:
**Sources:** **Intergroup conflict** — incompatible **goals** (speed vs quality), **scarce resources** (shared budget/time), possible **role ambiguity** over who has final sign-off.

**Functional potential:** Task conflict about **testing standards** can improve the launch if managed — but **relationship conflict** (personal emails) is **dysfunctional**.

**Thomas-Kilmann:** **Collaborating** fits — both **assertiveness** (each dept has valid stakes) and **cooperativeness** (shared company outcome). Explore **integrative solution**: limited fair release at trade fair with **beta units** while full launch follows testing.

**Avoid:** **Competing** (CEO picks marketing) leaves resentment; **Avoiding** lets damage spread.

**Process:** **Mediation** by neutral project manager; clear **role definition** for launch authority.

**Evaluation:** Collaboration takes **time** — under extreme deadline **compromising** (split delay) may be necessary. Link to **Tuckman storming** (4.3.1) — conflict is normal if teams move to norming afterward.
